pieces.label: Contextual Memory & Learning (CML) Framework — architecture diagram
pieces.note: Box-and-line ASCII architecture diagram of the multi-layer AI memory framework the three models converged on. Top node 'Contextual Memory & Learning Framework (CML)' branches into Memory Engine (CME) and User Control & Privacy Layer; the CME column descends through Session Layer (Ephemeral) -> Semantic Core (KG) -> Episodic Memory (Experience Store) -> Evolving User Model (Identity Graph) -> Forgetting Mechanism, with a parallel Consent Framework & Transparency box. Rendered inside a ```plaintext fence. The model intro'd it as 'a comprehensive ASCII representation of the memory framework we've discussed.' The message's trailing '### Explanation of Each' heading was cut off (transcript ends there).

missed_or_ambiguous: Only one visual-art piece in the thread: the CML framework box-drawing diagram in MSG 17 (matches header art_signal_msgs:1 / already_extracted_pieces:1). All other assistant messages (MSG 2,3,4,6,7,8,9,10,12,13,14,15) are prose/explanatory text — numbered lists with bold headers and indented bullets describing memory architectures, which is structured prose, not intentional visual art. The diagram is embedded among prose (intro sentence before the fence; an empty '### Explanation of Each' heading after it), so mode 'range' bounds just the art. first_line is the opening box border of the top CML node; last_line is the final '+-----------------------+' closing the Forgetting Mechanism box (the last line before the closing ``` fence). Note last_line '+-----------------------+' (23 dashes between the plus signs) is unique within the diagram — verified the Session Layer box uses a shorter border. Transcript appears truncated at MSG 17 (GPT-5's per-component explanation never rendered), but the ASCII diagram itself is complete and closed by its fence.
